title: Homer Alone

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening scene - Homer presumably encounters some frustration
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfectly character-integrated and economical, though not particularly original by this point in the series

jokes.text: I am not cleaning that. Mmm, who am I kidding?
jokes.setup: Marge declares she won't clean something
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself, knowing she inevitably will
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's resigned acceptance of her domestic role with quick self-correction

jokes.text: Now, Maggie... when you grow up... you can suck your pacifier all you want.
jokes.setup: Marge giving seemingly wise life advice to her baby
jokes.punchline: The 'wisdom' is about pacifier usage as an adult
jokes.explanation: Sweet absurdist moment showing Marge's loving but slightly misguided parenting philosophy

jokes.text: Overwhelming morning chaos with multiple demands
jokes.setup: Marge methodically making different sandwiches for each family member
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous barrage of conflicting demands and complaints
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for Marge's stress - the cacophony builds tension and sympathy for her situation

jokes.text: One at a time!
jokes.setup: Continued chaos with everyone talking over each other
jokes.punchline: Marge's desperate attempt to restore order
jokes.explanation: Natural parental reaction that many viewers will relate to

jokes.text: Bart, your hat's behind the toilet.
jokes.setup: Bart asking where his lucky red cap is
jokes.punchline: It's in the grossest possible location
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart - his prized possession ends up in the most inappropriate place

jokes.text: A bottle cap got lodged in the finger hole.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Marge to take his bowling ball to Nick's
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific and preventable problem
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer - somehow managing to damage his bowling ball in the most Homer-esque way

jokes.text: Ohh, alley balls?
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Homer use a ball at the bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Homer's disgusted reaction to using communal equipment
jokes.explanation: Great character beat - Homer's particular about his bowling despite being generally slovenly

jokes.text: No way. Never happen.
jokes.setup: Kids dismissively rejecting the possibility they'll miss the bus
jokes.punchline: Presumably they immediately miss the bus
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate ironic reversal - kids' overconfidence backfires

jokes.text: Well, I'll be damned.
jokes.setup: The kids' confident prediction
jokes.punchline: Homer's surprise at being proven wrong
jokes.explanation: Completes the simple irony setup - functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: It's a nervous twitch, and I'm sensitive about it... if you don't mind.
jokes.setup: Bart making faces at Lisa
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overly polite and clinical explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - turning a sibling squabble into a medical condition with formal politeness

jokes.text: Get... out!
jokes.setup: Continued family chaos and demands
jokes.punchline: Marge finally snapping
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation - builds toward Marge's eventual breakdown

jokes.text: Krusty brand duck sausage pizza
jokes.setup: Marge reading grocery items
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous Krusty-branded food combination
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurd product name - Krusty branding gone wrong

jokes.text: I haven't flushed a ball in years.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain his bowling ball problem
jokes.punchline: Nick's weirdly specific denial about flushing balls
jokes.explanation: Absurd denial that raises more questions than it answers - classic Simpsons randomness

jokes.text: Feels like there's a rat in my brain!
jokes.setup: Marge describing her stress headache
jokes.punchline: Vivid, disturbing imagery for a simple headache
jokes.explanation: Memorable description showing Marge's deteriorating mental state with graphic imagery

jokes.text: Your wife is dead... She just walked through a plate glass window. There's blood everywhere.
jokes.setup: Radio DJs doing a 'classic crank call'
jokes.punchline: Horrifically cruel prank about someone's wife dying
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that satirizes cruel radio pranks - uncomfortable but serves the show's satirical purpose

jokes.text: Noooo!!
jokes.setup: Accumulation of stressful sounds and demands
jokes.punchline: Marge's complete mental breakdown
jokes.explanation: Powerful culmination of built tension - Marge's breaking point effectively dramatized

jokes.text: And look out at 14th and Elm. I just dropped my bagel.
jokes.setup: Traffic reporter giving important traffic updates
jokes.punchline: Treats his dropped bagel as traffic news
jokes.explanation: Perfect media satire - trivial personal problem treated as news

jokes.text: I got a body in the trunk.
jokes.setup: Traffic jam with various frustrated drivers
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioned corpse as reason for urgency
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons dark humor - treating murder as mundane inconvenience

jokes.text: You've gotten chocolate all over everything.
jokes.setup: Following the body in trunk comment
jokes.punchline: Petty complaint about chocolate mess
jokes.explanation: Nice juxtaposition - major crime followed by minor domestic annoyance

jokes.text: Not getting enough of the good stuff?
jokes.setup: News reporter approaching distressed housewife for interview
jokes.punchline: Sleazy implication about marital relations
jokes.explanation: Typical sleazy reporter behavior - cheap laughs but fits character

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Marge's public breakdown on TV
jokes.punchline: His signature reaction to the crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect placement of Homer's catchphrase at the moment of crisis realization

jokes.text: That explains a lot.
jokes.setup: Homer identifying himself as Marge's husband
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's immediate understanding of why she snapped
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Wiggum and effective putdown of Homer

jokes.text: Don't put your lips on it.
jokes.setup: Wiggum giving Homer the police megaphone
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific hygiene concern at inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum - prioritizing minor concerns over major crisis

jokes.text: How about, 'Yes, it's me'?
jokes.setup: Homer asking what he should say to his wife
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's painfully obvious suggestion
jokes.explanation: Shows Wiggum helping with the obvious while Homer needs basic communication help

jokes.text: Later tonight, I think you and I should, uh... snuggle?
jokes.setup: Intimate moment between estranged spouses with live TV audience
jokes.punchline: Homer's awkward attempt at romance in worst possible context
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete inability to read the room - inappropriate timing and audience

jokes.text: Cuff her, boys!
jokes.setup: Touching reconciliation between Homer and Marge
jokes.punchline: Immediate arrest despite resolution
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - audience expects happy ending, gets harsh reality instead

jokes.text: She'll be cracking rocks by the end of the week.
jokes.setup: Wiggum being reassuring about Marge's situation
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate cheerful prediction
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum - trying to be helpful while making things worse

jokes.text: Quimby, don't write checks your butt can't cash.
jokes.setup: Political confrontation between Mayor Quimby and Chief Wiggum
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of 'don't write checks your mouth can't cash'
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum malapropism - anatomically incorrect but somehow more threatening

jokes.text: They're like seals-- toss 'em a fish... and watch 'em slap their fins together.
jokes.setup: Mayor observing the crowd's reaction to his proclamation
jokes.punchline: Cynical comparison of voters to trained seals
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire showing Quimby's contempt for voters he manipulates

jokes.text: They're like seals-- toss 'em a fish and watch 'em slap their fins together.
jokes.setup: Crowd applauding Quimby's political gesture
jokes.punchline: Cynical comparison of voters to performing seals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political cynicism - perfect metaphor for voter manipulation

jokes.text: Remember, you can't spell Relaxo without relax.
jokes.setup: Health spa commercial promoting relaxation
jokes.punchline: Painfully obvious wordplay that makes no real sense
jokes.explanation: Satirizes bad advertising copy - intentionally lazy wordplay that sounds profound but isn't

jokes.text: What?! You mean we're getting a divorce?
jokes.setup: Marge asking for a vacation by herself
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate jump to worst-case scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - can't conceive of middle ground between together and divorce

jokes.text: We've got six months of maternity leave we'll never use anyway.
jokes.setup: Twins offering to watch the kids
jokes.punchline: Dark reference to their childless status
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their spinster status - efficiently establishes character

jokes.text: Wish I'd thought of that.
jokes.setup: Seeing how Patty and Selma handle Maggie (presumably with some clever method)
jokes.punchline: Homer's admission of inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat showing Homer recognizing better parenting when he sees it

jokes.text: He'll probably trade her... for a beer and a nudie magazine.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Homer watching Maggie alone
jokes.punchline: Patty's harsh but possibly accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty - cruel but specific insult that feels believable given Homer's priorities

jokes.text: See? Got her on the first bounce.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his ability to care for Maggie
jokes.punchline: Immediately drops her but treats it as success
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy - Homer turning his failure into claimed success

jokes.text: See? Got her on the first bounce.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to prove he can handle Maggie
jokes.punchline: Apparently drops and catches the baby like a ball
jokes.explanation: Dark physical comedy - Homer treating baby like sports equipment, confirming the twins' fears

jokes.text: Don't!
jokes.setup: Homer asking how to use the pressure cooker
jokes.punchline: Marge's immediate, panicked response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - single word conveys Marge's terror at Homer with dangerous appliances

jokes.text: You haven't touched your tongue sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Aunt serving dinner to the kids
jokes.punchline: Revolting menu item served casually
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disgusting food from the twins - shows how different they are from Marge

jokes.text: You think you know fear? Well, I've seen them naked.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying she's scared about staying with the aunts
jokes.punchline: Bart's traumatic trump card
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Bart's seen horrors Lisa can't imagine

jokes.text: You might remember me from such films as... Today We Kill, Tomorrow We Die, and Gladys, the Groovy Mule.
jokes.setup: Troy McClure introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Absurd fake movie titles
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure introduction with perfectly absurd fake filmography

jokes.text: That can't be good for me. It tastes too good.
jokes.setup: Tasting the low-calorie spa food
jokes.punchline: Common assumption that healthy food can't taste good
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation about healthy food expectations - solid but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, Gregory... where have you been all my life?
jokes.setup: Marge watching soap opera
jokes.punchline: Getting emotionally invested in cheesy romance
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Marge's romantic side emerging during her getaway

jokes.text: Mmm. Strained peas. [To Tune Of 'Rule Brittania'] # Shakespeare's fried chicken #
jokes.setup: Homer trying to feed Maggie while watching TV
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous commercial jingle for Shakespeare-themed fried chicken
jokes.explanation: Absurd corporate branding - Shakespeare's fried chicken is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: D'oh! Let go!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to feed Maggie
jokes.punchline: Baby presumably grabbing and not letting go
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy showing Homer's inexperience with baby care

jokes.text: D'oh! Let go!
jokes.setup: Homer and Maggie in some kind of physical struggle
jokes.punchline: Fourth 'D'oh!' combined with baby-related mishap
jokes.explanation: Another well-placed 'D'oh!' with physical comedy element - Homer vs. baby dynamics

jokes.text: Oh, it's me.
jokes.setup: Barney commenting that someone smells stinky
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's the source
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment - self-awareness about his hygiene issues

jokes.text: Ay, caramba.!
jokes.setup: Bart looking through the aunts' belongings
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase in response to something shocking
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart catchphrase perfectly deployed when he finds something inappropriate

jokes.text: I said it before, and I'll say it again. Ay, caramba.!
jokes.setup: Bart finding something shocking
jokes.punchline: Self-conscious delivery of his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment - Bart acknowledging his own catchphrase usage

jokes.text: Aw, Bart... that's a blackhead gun!
jokes.setup: Bart playing with something he found
jokes.punchline: It's a disgusting beauty tool
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out comedy - innocent play item turns disgusting

jokes.text: Richard Dean Anderson will be in my dreams tonight.
jokes.setup: Watching MacGyver on TV
jokes.punchline: Aunt's romantic fixation on the star
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - the twins' specific celebrity crushes

jokes.text: You want a bad night? Try sleeping... on one of these.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about his bad experience
jokes.punchline: Barney's one-upmanship about sleeping conditions
jokes.explanation: Typical Barney - competing over who has it worse

jokes.text: I make them with two kinds of cheese.
jokes.setup: Homer panicking about losing Maggie
jokes.punchline: Barney's completely inappropriate focus on omelet quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney - completely missing the crisis while focusing on cooking details

jokes.text: The sooner I get her out... the sooner we can have omelets.
jokes.setup: Trying to help rescue the baby from a dangerous situation
jokes.punchline: His motivation is still just wanting breakfast
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Barney's food obsession during baby rescue - perfect character consistency

jokes.text: I can't even put a bag over my head.
jokes.setup: Marge looking at herself in spa mirror
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating comment about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Sweet self-deprecating humor showing Marge's insecurity

jokes.text: Let's get out of here, Thelma. Okay, Louise.
jokes.setup: Marge ordering room service including tequila
jokes.punchline: Reference to Thelma & Louise movie
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference showing Marge's rebellious fantasy

jokes.text: Come on, kids. It's time to rub Aunt Patty's feet.
jokes.setup: Kids visiting with their aunts
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable request for foot massage from children
jokes.explanation: Appropriately uncomfortable aunt behavior - making kids do gross adult tasks

jokes.text: Lube job while you wait? Don't touch me.
jokes.setup: Friendly service offer at gas station
jokes.punchline: Homer's paranoid reaction
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo with Homer's typical overreaction

jokes.text: Well, good. The dog didn't run away.
jokes.setup: Homer asking how Marge would feel if the dog ran away
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was testing the waters for bad news about losing Maggie
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer strategy - testing reaction to mild bad news before revealing worse news

jokes.text: Please, don't jump. I know you're going through some tough times...
jokes.setup: Homer on bridge ledge looking for Maggie
jokes.punchline: Someone assumes he's suicidal
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience knows Homer's real purpose but stranger doesn't

jokes.text: Maggie was very young. It's not like we got so attached to her.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort Marge about losing Maggie
jokes.punchline: Horrifically inappropriate logic about baby attachment
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny showing Homer's complete failure to understand parenthood

jokes.text: Just don't do it again, you big lug.
jokes.setup: Homer thanking officer profusely for finding Maggie
jokes.punchline: Casual warning about child endangerment
jokes.explanation: Nice understatement treating serious neglect as minor mistake

jokes.text: I think I speak... for everyone in this bed... when I say you have nothing to worry about.
jokes.setup: Family all crowded in bed with Marge
jokes.punchline: Bart's formal diplomatic language in intimate setting
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending - Bart speaking formally for the group in loving family moment

tmdb_overview: A stressed Marge vacations alone, leaving Bart and Lisa with Patty and Selma, and Maggie with Homer--until she leaves in search of Marge.
insight: Marge's spa escape frames 64 jokes at steady 4.3-per-minute pace, anchored by character comedy.
